responses.text: The 2021 Esquire article reports on William "Rick" Singer, the mastermind behind the "Operation Varsity Blues" college admissions scheme.
At the time of publication, the article correctly stated that Singer was awaiting sentencing after pleading guilty in 2019 and cooperating with investigators.
The article also noted that Singer briefly enrolled in a doctorate program at Grand Canyon University in 2019 before dropping out in July 2020.
However, the article's core premise regarding his status is now outdated.
On January 4, 2023, Singer was sentenced to three and a half years in federal prison for orchestrating the sweeping bribery and test-cheating fraud.
He was additionally ordered to pay approximately $19 million, split between restitution to the IRS and asset forfeitures to the federal government.
Prosecutors emphasized his role as the primary architect of the multi-million dollar enterprise, culminating in the longest sentence handed down to any defendant involved in the scandal.
